report:
EcoHarbor Technologies – Private Equity Investment Memo
Date: April 12, 2026

I. Company and Business Model Overview

– EcoHarbor Technologies, founded in 2018 and headquartered in Seattle, WA, develops and operates modular floating solar power platforms designed for deployment on reservoirs, lakes, and coastal protected waters.
– The company provides end-to-end solutions including design, manufacturing, installation, and O&M (operations & maintenance) services.
– Business model:
  • Hardware sales/licensing: Modular solar float platform units sold to utility customers and independent power producers.
  • Long-term service contracts: 10- to 20-year O&M contracts with fixed and variable components.
  • Project development partnerships: Participates as minority equity partner in select utility-scale solar projects (>30 MW capacity).

– Scale & milestones:
  • Installed base as of FY25 (year ended Dec 31, 2025): 480 MW capacity across 12 projects in North America and Europe.
  • Annual revenue FY25: $142.3M (+62% YoY vs $87.9M in FY24).
  • Gross margin FY25: 41.5%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA FY25: $29.2M (20.5% margin).

– Recent funding & ownership:
  • Completed Series C in Q1 2026 raising $75M, led by GreenWave Capital, valuing the company at $460M pre-money.
  • Existing investors include Energize Ventures and BrightFuture PE.

II. Market Opportunity and Industry Context

– Floating solar market projected CAGR of 20.1% through 2035 globally, driven by scarcity of land real estate, growing renewable mandates, and need for efficient water surface solar installations.
– EcoHarbor’s target customers include utilities, municipal water authorities, large industrial water reservoir owners, and independent renewable energy developers.
– Regulatory tailwinds:
  • Increasing renewable portfolio standards (RPS) across U.S. and Europe.
  • Incentives for clean energy infrastructure including ITC (Investment Tax Credit) renewal through 2032.

III. Competitive Landscape

– Direct competitors:
  • AquaSolar Inc.: Focuses on floating solar for small-scale applications; limited modularity.
  • SunFloat Energy: European-based, strong installation services but limited hardware innovation.
  • HydroArray Tech: Early-stage startup focused on freshwater bodies; technology less mature.

– EcoHarbor advantages:
  • Proprietary modular design reduces installation time by ~35% compared to peers.
  • Patented anchoring systems optimizing stability in varying water conditions.
  • Vertical integration allowing control over component supply chain.

– Challenges:
  • High capital expenditure per MW vs. fixed-tilt ground solar (~15–20% premium).
  • Emerging market with some site permitting complexities.

IV. Customer Base and Contracts

– Over 20 utility clients and municipal authorities have placed purchase and service contracts.
– Contract structure:
  • 70% of revenue from hardware sales with upfront payments.
  • 30% recurring revenue from O&M, growing annually as base expands.
  • Low customer concentration: largest client accounts for 12% of FY25 revenue.

– Retention:
  • High renewal rates on O&M contracts (>90% over last 3 years).
  • Strong customer satisfaction ratings reported in post-project surveys.

V. Financial Overview

FY24 and FY25 (USD millions):

| Metric            | FY24       | FY25       | YoY Growth |
|-------------------|------------|------------|------------|
| Revenue           | 87.9       | 142.3      | +62%       |
| Gross Profit      | 34.5       | 59.0       | +71%       |
| Gross Margin (%)  | 39.3       | 41.5       | +220 bps   |
| Adj. EBITDA       | 14.3       | 29.2       | +104%      |
| EBITDA Margin (%) | 16.3       | 20.5       | +420 bps   |
| Net Income        | (2.8)      | 5.1        | NMF        |

– Balance Sheet highlights (end FY25):
  • Cash: $45.2M
  • Debt: $18.5M (primarily equipment financing)
  • Working Capital: $29.7M

– Free Cash Flow FY25: $22.4M, positive and growing.

VI. Growth Strategies

– Geographic expansion into Asia-Pacific where floating solar is nascent but with regulatory support.
– Increase project development partnerships to supplement hardware sales and services.
– R&D investment targeting next-gen higher efficiency panels integration and floating storage solutions.
– Operational scaling to achieve $250M+ revenue run-rate by 2028 with 25% EBITDA margins.

VII. Risks and Mitigations

– Supply chain disruptions in solar PV panels: Company maintains diversified supplier base and holds 3 months inventory.
– Permitting delays: In-house regulatory team and partnership with local engineering firms.
– Competition from ground-mounted solar and offshore wind: Focus on niche sites where floating solar is uniquely advantageous.

VIII. Summary

EcoHarbor Technologies is a rapidly growing, vertically integrated floating solar technology company with a strong technology moat, diversified blue-chip customer base, and demonstrated financial progress toward profitability and cash flow positivity. The company is well-positioned to capitalize on the fast-growing global floating solar market with tangible growth levers and operational improvements. 

----

This memo is for informational and preliminary investment consideration only and does not constitute an offer or recommendation.
reasoning:
Okay, looking at this company EcoHarbor Technologies, I see we're dealing with a cleantech innovator focused on floating solar platforms, which is a niche but fast-growing segment in renewable energy. Let me analyze the financials here. The company has shown strong revenue growth of 62% year-over-year, reaching $142 million in FY25, which is impressive. Their gross margins are improving too, up over 220 basis points to 41.5%, indicating better cost control and possibly scale advantages with their modular designs.

The EBITDA margin more than doubled from 16.3% to 20.5%, showing strong operating leverage as they scale. They’ve also moved from a net loss to positive net income in FY25 and generated meaningful free cash flow ($22.4 million), pointing to improving profitability and cash flow quality.

Competition is moderate but manageable. EcoHarbor's proprietary technology — modular floating solar platforms and patented anchoring — seem to give it a defensible moat. The competitors listed are either less technologically advanced or smaller scale. This vertical integration over supply chain also helps mitigate input cost risks.

Customer-wise, they have over 20 utility and municipal clients with low customer concentration, which is good for revenue diversity and risk mitigation. Long-term service contracts create recurring revenue and high retention (>90%), which boosts revenue visibility and valuation multiple potential.

Market opportunity is substantial given accelerating renewables mandates and the land scarcity limiting traditional solar deployment. Floating solar is expected to grow at 20% CAGR globally, which supports multiple years of strong top-line growth.

Growth drivers include geographic expansion into Asia-Pacific, increasing project-level equity partnerships, and new product development related to higher efficiency panels and integrated storage.

Risks include supply chain parts availability and permitting delays, but the company has proactive mitigation strategies.

Overall, this company seems well-positioned in a growing segment with solid technology leadership, strong financial momentum, and multiple avenues for growth. This aligns perfectly with a strong investment opportunity profile.

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. Strong revenue growth of 62% YoY with improving gross and EBITDA margins, demonstrating scalable profitable growth.
2. Proprietary modular floating solar technology with patented anchoring systems provides significant competitive moat.
3. Diversified blue-chip customer base with high contract retention rates, leading to recurring revenue streams.
4. Favorable market tailwinds due to increasing renewable mandates and land scarcity boosting floating solar adoption.
5. Vertical integration and supply chain management mitigate input cost and availability risks.
6. Clear growth strategies including geographic expansion, project partnerships, and next-gen product development.
7. Positive net income and growing free cash flow indicate strong financial health and capital efficiency.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Floating solar has a 15–20% higher capital cost vs. fixed-tilt ground solar, potentially limiting adoption in price-sensitive markets.
2. Some regulatory and permitting risks in emerging geographies which could delay projects.
3. Competition from established solar market players and alternative renewables like offshore wind.
4. Market adoption is still early-stage, requiring sustained customer education and infrastructure development.
5. Dependence on continued government incentives and renewable energy policies which may evolve.
6. Supply chain vulnerabilities, particularly for solar panel components, though partially mitigated by diversified suppliers.
verdict.similar_deals:
— GreenTech Capital’s $320M growth equity investment in SunSeas Floating Solar in 2024.
— BrightFuture PE’s $150M buyout of HydroPanel Solutions specializing in innovative solar tech (2023).
— Energize Ventures’ $90M Series B lead in AquaFloats, a floating solar platform maker (2022).
— OceanPower Growth Fund’s $200M minority investment in CleanWave Renewables, a hybrid offshore solar/wind developer (2025).
verdict.key_information_requested:
— Detailed pipeline and sales visibility over next 24 months, including project approval stages.
— Breakdown of cost structure and expected improvements in hardware margin with scale.
— Client contract terms and renewal history, especially O&M agreement duration and pricing.
— Technology roadmap and R&D investment plans for next 3 years.
— Sensitivity analysis on margins and cash flow under different subsidy scenarios.
— Competitive intelligence on new entrants and potential substitution risks.
